title: Unused private methods should be removed
summary: A private method can only be invoked from inside the type that declares it. When no reference to it exists anywhere in that type, it is dead code: it cannot be reached, yet it still has to be read, maintained and compiled.

This rule reports a private method whose name appears nowhere else in the declaring type. If the method is genuinely an entry point reached by reflection, give it a usage or document it so the intent is explicit.
